response.justification: Candidate has broad full-stack experience spanning 15+ years with current Staff Software Engineer title at re:cinq, including recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling which aligns with the AI-native engineering mindset. However, the resume shows Python is absent from the listed skills despite being a must-have, and distributed systems / high-throughput experience is limited to one role (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) several years ago. Strong craftsmanship signals (TDD, teaching, open-source) partially offset the technical gaps, warranting a screening call to verify Python depth and current distributed systems competency.
strengths: Current Staff title with hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generator work — matches the AI-native baseline expectation
Demonstrated craftsmanship culture: TDD, pairing, trunk-based development, plus teaching workshops and conference talks
Prior high-throughput experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests/sec with low-latency async architecture
concerns: Python is not listed in skills — primary stack is TypeScript/Node and DLang, which is a direct miss on a must-have
Distributed systems depth is concentrated in a 2018–2020 role; recent work is product/full-stack rather than high-throughput architecture
No clear evidence of stakeholder management at scale, on-call/incident-response ownership, or technical strategy leadership across a domain
